behavioral_patterns.value:
Pre-patch exploitation followed by delayed remediation

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Adversaries weaponize vulnerabilities before patches exist
  • Step 2: Organizations discover vulnerability through disclosure
  • Step 3: Manual remediation processes take months to complete
  • Step 4: Long-tail assets remain exposed extending average remediation time

Detection: Monitor for exploitation attempts of recently disclosed vulnerabilities and track remediation progress against weaponization timelines
behavioral_patterns.context: Pre-patch exploitation followed by delayed remediation

behavioral_patterns.value:
AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: AI agents discover vulnerabilities autonomously
  • Step 2: AI systems weaponize exploits faster than human timelines
  • Step 3: AI executes attacks at machine speed
  • Step 4: Human defenders respond at significantly slower pace

Detection: Implement machine-speed detection systems that can identify rapid vulnerability discovery and exploitation patterns characteristic of AI-driven attacks

Manual tax exploitation pattern

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Attackers identify long-tail assets with slower remediation cycles
  • Step 2: Focus exploitation on infrastructure systems with extended patch windows
  • Step 3: Maintain persistence on manually-managed assets
  • Step 4: Leverage extended exposure windows for lateral movement

Detection: Monitor infrastructure systems and long-tail assets for signs of exploitation, especially those with historically slow remediation cycles

Risk mass accumulation attack strategy

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Identify vulnerabilities with high remediation complexity
  • Step 2: Target multiple vulnerable assets simultaneously
  • Step 3: Exploit during extended exposure windows
  • Step 4: Maximize impact through cumulative exposure across environment

Detection: Track cumulative exposure metrics and monitor for coordinated exploitation attempts across multiple vulnerable assets

Pre-disclosure exploitation window abuse

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Discover zero-day vulnerabilities before public disclosure
  • Step 2: Develop exploits during blind spot period
  • Step 3: Deploy attacks before patches are available
  • Step 4: Maintain access through disclosure and initial remediation phases

Detection: Implement behavioral analytics to detect unusual activity patterns that may indicate zero-day exploitation before public disclosure

Scan-and-report model bypass

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Identify organizations using traditional vulnerability management
  • Step 2: Target vulnerabilities during manual routing delays
  • Step 3: Exploit during ticket processing and approval phases
  • Step 4: Maintain persistence through slow manual remediation cycles

Detection: Monitor for exploitation attempts that correlate with vulnerability scanning cycles and manual remediation workflows

Infrastructure system targeting pattern

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Identify critical infrastructure systems with extended patch cycles
  • Step 2: Focus on assets with median remediation times over 200 days
  • Step 3: Establish persistent access during long exposure windows
  • Step 4: Use infrastructure access for broader network compromise

Detection: Implement enhanced monitoring for critical infrastructure systems with known long remediation cycles, focusing on early indicators of compromise

Theoretical vs exploitable vulnerability misdirection

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Generate noise through theoretical vulnerability reports
  • Step 2: Overwhelm remediation teams with low-priority tickets
  • Step 3: Exploit genuinely weaponized vulnerabilities while teams focus on theoretical risks
  • Step 4: Maintain access through misprioritized remediation efforts

Detection: Prioritize monitoring and response for actively weaponized vulnerabilities over theoretical exposures, using threat intelligence to guide focus

Human ceiling exploitation timing

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Analyze target organization's remediation capacity and timelines
  • Step 2: Time attacks to coincide with peak vulnerability volumes
  • Step 3: Exploit during periods when human teams are overwhelmed
  • Step 4: Leverage structural limitations in manual processes

Detection: Monitor for increased exploitation attempts during high-volume vulnerability disclosure periods when remediation teams are likely overwhelmed

Average window of exposure maximization

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Target vulnerabilities with historically long remediation tails
  • Step 2: Exploit during pre-disclosure blind spots
  • Step 3: Maintain persistence through disclosure and initial response
  • Step 4: Leverage extended exposure windows for maximum impact

Detection: Track full exposure windows from weaponization to complete remediation and monitor for exploitation attempts throughout extended exposure periods

-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 4 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Behavior: AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration

Behavio
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-f1ff2ca8
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: AI agents discover vulnerabilities' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: AI agents discover vulnerabilities
-- Source: behavioral_1
-- TTP: T1082
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: AI agents discover vulnerabilities' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1082' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_1' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bAI\-powered\ autonomous\ attack\ acceleration\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ AI\ agents\ discover\ vulnerabilities\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bAI\-powered\ autonomous\ attack\ acceleration\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ AI\ agents\ discover\ vulnerabilities\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 10 rules from 10 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Pre-patch exploitation followed by delayed remediation
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration
// Category: discovery
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1082
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where FileName in~ ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| summarize ReconCount = count(),
    UniqueTools = dcount(FileName),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ReconCount >= 3 or UniqueTools >= 2
| sort by ReconCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Manual tax exploitation pattern
// Category: lateral_movement
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1021
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where RemotePort in (445, 3389, 5985, 5986, 22)
| summarize ConnectionCount = count(),
    UniqueTargets = dcount(RemoteIP),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemotePort
| where UniqueTargets >= 3
| sort by UniqueTargets desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Risk mass accumulation attack strategy
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Pre-disclosure exploitation window abuse
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Scan-and-report model bypass
// Category: persistence
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1547
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
| where RegistryKey has "CurrentVersion\\Run"
| summarize ModCount = count(),
    UniqueKeys = dcount(RegistryKey),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| sort by ModCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Infrastructure system targeting pattern
// Category: persistence
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
| where RegistryKey has "CurrentVersion\\Run"
| summarize ModCount = count(),
    UniqueKeys = dcount(RegistryKey),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| sort by ModCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Theoretical vs exploitable vulnerability misdirection
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 9: Behavioral Detection - Human ceiling exploitation timing
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 10: Behavioral Detection - Average window of exposure maximization
// Category: persistence
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1547
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
| where RegistryKey has "CurrentVersion\\Run"
| summarize ModCount = count(),
    UniqueKeys = dcount(RegistryKey),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName
| sort by ModCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 10 rules from 10 indicators

``` Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Pre-patch exploitation followed by delayed remediation
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-powered autonomous attack acceleration
``` Category: discovery
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1082
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| stats count as recon_count, dc(process_name) as unique_tools, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where recon_count >= 3 OR unique_tools >= 2
| sort -recon_count
| table dest, user, recon_count, unique_tools, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Manual tax exploitation pattern
``` Category: lateral_movement
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1021
index=main sourcetype=network:traffic dest_port IN (445, 3389, 5985, 5986, 22)
| stats count as conn_count, dc(dest_ip) as unique_targets, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, user, dest_port
| where unique_targets >= 3
| sort -unique_targets
| table src_ip, user, dest_port, conn_count, unique_targets, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Risk mass accumulation attack strategy
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - Pre-disclosure exploitation window abuse
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Scan-and-report model bypass
``` Category: persistence
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1547
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:registry registry_path="*CurrentVersion\\Run*" action=modified
| stats count as mod_count, dc(registry_key) as unique_keys, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user, process_name
| sort -mod_count
| table dest, user, process_name, mod_count, unique_keys, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Infrastructure system targeting pattern
``` Category: persistence
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:registry registry_path="*CurrentVersion\\Run*" action=modified
| stats count as mod_count, dc(registry_key) as unique_keys, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user, process_name
| sort -mod_count
| table dest, user, process_name, mod_count, unique_keys, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Theoretical vs exploitable vulnerability misdirection
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 9: Behavioral Detection - Human ceiling exploitation timing
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure

compiled_outputs.sigma:
# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 10 rules from 10 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - Pre-patch exploitation followed by delayed remediation'
id: e04d860c-116a-52c4-890b-41f714429bdf
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
logsource:
    category: authentication
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        status: 'failure'
    filter_low_count:
        count|gte: 5
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

---
